---
title: "Detainers, post- de-duplication"
author: "Cindy J. Pang"
date: "2026-03-04"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
rm(list=ls()) 
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(arrow)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
```

# Compare Dropped Counts to Original
```{r}
detainers_data <- read_feather(here::here("data/ice-processed/detainers-merged-with-flags.feather"))|>
  filter(drop_row == FALSE)|>
  filter(!is.na(Prepare_Date)) |>
  mutate(
    week_start = floor_date(Prepare_Date, unit = "week", week_start = 7), # Sunday
    year = year(week_start),
    week = week(week_start),
    year_week = sprintf("%d-W%02d", year, week)
  ) |>
  arrange(week_start)

detainers_counts_original <- read_feather(here::here("data/ice-counts/detainers-weekly-counts.feather"))|>
  rename(n0 = n)

weekly_counts <- detainers_data |>
  group_by(year_week, week_start) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_source_files = n_distinct(source_file),
    source_files = paste(sort(unique(source_file)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(week_start)

compare_counts <- merge(weekly_counts, 
  detainers_counts_original, 
  by = c("year_week", "week_start"), 
  all = TRUE)|>
  mutate(
    n_dropped = n0-n
  )

# Visual Inspection of Counts - counts look like they mostly overlap
ggplot(compare_counts, aes(x=week_start))+
  geom_line(aes(y=n0, color = "Original Count"), alpha = 0.5)+
  geom_line(aes(y=n, color = "Dropped Count"), alpha = 0.5)+
  labs(
    x = "Week", 
    y = "Counts", 
    title = "Comparison of Dropped Counts vs. Original Counts"
  )+
  theme_minimal()
```

The dropped counts and original seem to mostly overlap (no big concerns/issues)  

# Plot of Removals (post-duplication drops)  
```{r}
df <- compare_counts |>
  arrange(week_start) |>
  mutate(
    source_files_norm = ifelse(is.na(source_files), "", source_files),
    changed = source_files_norm != lag(source_files_norm, default = first(source_files_norm)),
    seg_id = cumsum(changed),
    seg_code = paste0("S", seg_id)
  )

# segment metadata (for label placement)
segments <- df |>
  group_by(seg_id, seg_code) |>
  summarise(
    seg_start = min(week_start),
    seg_end   = max(week_start),
    seg_mid   = seg_start + as.numeric(seg_end - seg_start)/2,
    source_files = first(source_files_norm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# vlines at segment starts (exclude first segment)
vlines <- segments |>
  arrange(seg_start) |>
  slice(-1)

# y position for segment codes (near top)
y_top <- max(df$n0, df$n_dropped, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(df, aes(x = week_start)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = n), linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(
    data = vlines,
    aes(xintercept = as.numeric(seg_start)),
    linetype = "dashed",
    alpha = 0.6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = segments,
    aes(x = seg_mid, y = y_top, label = seg_code),
    vjust = -0.2,
    size = 3,
    label.size = 0.2,
    alpha = 0.95,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Removals with Data Source Change Points",
    subtitle = "See Segment Codes (SX) to see time periods",
    x = "Week start",
    y = "Counts",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

segments_print <- segments |>
  select(seg_code, seg_start, seg_end, source_files)
knitr::kable(segments_print, col.names = c("Segment Code", "Start Date", "End Date", "Source Files"))
```

# Weekly Detainers by Source File  
```{r}
weekly_counts_by_src <- detainers_data |>
  group_by(source_file, year_week, week_start) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(source_file, week_start)

ggplot(weekly_counts_by_src, 
       aes(x = week_start, y = n, color = source_file)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Detainer Counts by Source File",
    x = "Week Start",
    y = "Number of Detainers",
    color = "Source File"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

It looks like the only files we need would be `2023_ICFO_42034` and `120125` past a certain point.  

## Drop other source files for final dataset  
```{r}
detainers_data_final <- detainers_data |>
  mutate(keep_row = case_when(
    source_file == "npr" & Prepare_Date < as.Date("2017-06-18") ~ TRUE, 
    source_file == "2025-ICFO-18038" & Prepare_Date >= as.Date("2017-06-18") & Prepare_Date < as.Date("2025-03-16")~ TRUE, 
    source_file == "120125" & Prepare_Date >= as.Date("2025-03-16") ~ TRUE, 
    TRUE ~ FALSE
  ))|>
  filter(keep_row == TRUE)

weekly_counts_by_src <- detainers_data_final |>
  group_by(source_file, week_start) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(source_file, week_start)

ggplot(weekly_counts_by_src, 
       aes(x = week_start, y = n, color = source_file)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Detainer Counts by Source File",
    x = "Week Start",
    y = "Number of Detainers",
    color = "Source File"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r, eval=F}
detainers_data_final <- detainers_data_final |>
  select(-drop_row, -year, -week, -year_week, -duplicate_likely, -keep_row)

write_feather(detainers_data_final, "data/ice-final/detainers-final.feather")
```
